x-inspectra.cliParsedDescription: Meziantou Dev Commands - A CLI for managing git repositories

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --version
options.description: Show version information

options.name: --config-file
options.description: Path to the configuration file

commands.name: activate
commands.description: Activate an eligible Azure PIM role for the current user

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format: text, json, table, list

options.name: --role
options.description: Role name or role definition ID to activate when multiple eligible roles exist

options.name: --duration
options.description: Activation duration in ISO 8601 format (default: PT1H) [default: PT1H]

options.name: --justification
options.description: Justification for activation (optional)

options.name: --ticket-number
options.description: Ticket number for activation (optional)

options.name: --ticket-system
options.description: Ticket system name for activation (optional)

arguments.name: resource
arguments.description: Azure resource URL or subscription ID

commands.name: edit
commands.description: Open the configuration file in the default editor

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format: text, json, table, list

commands.name: show
commands.description: Show configuration information

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format: text, json, table, list

commands.name: list
commands.description: List dependencies found in a repository

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format: text, json, table, list

options.name: --dependency-type
options.description: Filter dependencies by type (e.g., NuGet, Npm). Can be specified multiple times

options.name: --dependency-name
options.description: Filter dependencies by name using a case-insensitive glob pattern (e.g., Meziantou.*)

options.name: --worktrees-only
options.description: Only include worktrees (exclude main repositories) [default: False]

options.name: --no-worktrees
options.description: Exclude worktrees (only include main repositories) [default: False]

options.name: --github-forks-only
options.description: Only include fork repositories from GitHub [default: False]

options.name: --github-exclude-forks
options.description: Exclude fork repositories from GitHub [default: False]

options.name: --github-include-archived
options.description: Include archived repositories from GitHub (excluded by default) [default: False]

options.name: --github-archived-only
options.description: Only include archived repositories from GitHub [default: False]

options.name: --github-private-only
options.description: Only include private repositories from GitHub [default: False]

options.name: --github-public-only
options.description: Only include public repositories from GitHub [default: False]

arguments.name: repositories
arguments.description: The repository to operate on (name, URL, path, or .code-workspace file). Supports globbing patterns (e.g., 'meziantou.*' or '*' for all). Supports source prefixes: 'local:' (local only), 'gh:' or 'github:' (GitHub only). For explicit GitHub inputs without owner (e.g., 'gh:sample'), default owners from configuration are used. Multiple values can be separated by semicolons. Prefix with '!' to exclude.

commands.name: update
commands.description: Update dependencies found in a repository

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format: text, json, table, list

options.name: --dependency-type
options.description: Filter dependencies by type (e.g., NuGet, Npm). Can be specified multiple times

options.name: --files
options.description: Glob patterns to select files to scan for dependencies (relative to the repository root)

options.name: --update-lock-files
options.description: Update lock files after dependencies are updated [default: False]

options.name: --worktrees-only
options.description: Only include worktrees (exclude main repositories) [default: False]

options.name: --no-worktrees
options.description: Exclude worktrees (only include main repositories) [default: False]

options.name: --github-forks-only
options.description: Only include fork repositories from GitHub [default: False]

options.name: --github-exclude-forks

options.name: --github-include-archived
options.description: Include archived repositories from GitHub (excluded by default) [default: False]

options.name: --github-archived-only
options.description: Only include archived repositories from GitHub [default: False]

options.name: --github-private-only
options.description: Only include private repositories from GitHub [default: False]

options.name: --github-public-only
options.description: Only include public repositories from GitHub [default: False]

arguments.name: repositories
arguments.description: The repository to operate on (name, URL, path, or .code-workspace file). Supports globbing patterns (e.g., 'meziantou.*' or '*' for all). Supports source prefixes: 'local:' (local only), 'gh:' or 'github:' (GitHub only). For explicit GitHub inputs without owner (e.g., 'gh:sample'), default owners from configuration are used. Multiple values can be separated by semicolons. Prefix with '!' to exclude.

commands.name: list
commands.description: List repositories from local and GitHub sources

options.description: Output format: text, json, table, list

arguments.description: Repositories to list. Supports globbing (e.g., 'sample*' or '*'), source prefixes ('local:' or 'gh:'), semicolon-separated values, and exclusions with '!'. For explicit GitHub inputs without owner (e.g., 'gh:sample'), default owners from configuration are used.

commands.name: create
commands.description: Create a new git worktree for a repository

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format: text, json, table, list

options.name: --name
options.description: Custom name for the worktree folder (defaults to branch name)

options.name: --new-branch
options.description: Create a new branch with the specified name [default: False]

options.name: --source
options.description: The source commit, branch, or tag to create the new branch from (defaults to origin/default-branch or HEAD if no remote)

options.name: --force
options.description: Force creation even if the branch is already checked out elsewhere [default: False]

options.name: --open-editor
options.description: Open the new worktree folder in the default editor [default: True]

options.name: --worktrees-only
options.description: Only include worktrees (exclude main repositories) [default: False]

options.name: --no-worktrees
options.description: Exclude worktrees (only include main repositories) [default: False]

options.name: --github-forks-only
options.description: Only include fork repositories from GitHub [default: False]

options.name: --github-exclude-forks
options.description: Exclude fork repositories from GitHub [default: False]

options.name: --github-include-archived
options.description: Include archived repositories from GitHub (excluded by default) [default: False]

options.name: --github-archived-only
options.description: Only include archived repositories from GitHub [default: False]

options.name: --github-private-only
options.description: Only include private repositories from GitHub [default: False]

options.name: --github-public-only
options.description: Only include public repositories from GitHub [default: False]

arguments.name: repositories
arguments.description: The repository to operate on (name, URL, path, or .code-workspace file). Supports globbing patterns (e.g., 'meziantou.*' or '*' for all). Supports source prefixes: 'local:' (local only), 'gh:' or 'github:' (GitHub only). For explicit GitHub inputs without owner (e.g., 'gh:sample'), default owners from configuration are used. Multiple values can be separated by semicolons. Prefix with '!' to exclude.

arguments.name: branch
arguments.description: The branch, commit, PR URL (e.g., https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/123), or PR number to checkout

commands.name: domain
commands.description: Get the Microsoft Entra tenant ID for a domain

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format: text, json, table, list

options.name: --openid-configuration
options.description: Raw OpenID configuration JSON (skips the network call)

arguments.name: domain
arguments.description: Domain name to resolve

commands.name: random
commands.description: Generate a random number

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format: text, json, table, list

options.name: --min
options.description: Minimum value (inclusive)

options.name: --max
options.description: Maximum value (exclusive)

commands.name: vs
commands.description: Open a repository in Visual Studio

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format: text, json, table, list

options.name: --worktrees-only
options.description: Only include worktrees (exclude main repositories) [default: False]

options.name: --no-worktrees
options.description: Exclude worktrees (only include main repositories) [default: False]

options.name: --github-forks-only
options.description: Only include fork repositories from GitHub [default: False]

options.name: --github-exclude-forks
options.description: Exclude fork repositories from GitHub [default: False]

options.name: --github-include-archived
options.description: Include archived repositories from GitHub (excluded by default) [default: False]

options.name: --github-archived-only
options.description: Only include archived repositories from GitHub [default: False]

options.name: --github-private-only
options.description: Only include private repositories from GitHub [default: False]

options.name: --github-public-only
options.description: Only include public repositories from GitHub [default: False]

arguments.name: repositories
arguments.description: The repository to operate on (name, URL, path, or .code-workspace file). Supports globbing patterns (e.g., 'meziantou.*' or '*' for all). Supports source prefixes: 'local:' (local only), 'gh:' or 'github:' (GitHub only). For explicit GitHub inputs without owner (e.g., 'gh:sample'), default owners from configuration are used. Multiple values can be separated by semicolons. Prefix with '!' to exclude.

commands.name: create
commands.description: Create a VS Code workspace from a set of repositories and open it

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format: text, json, table, list

options.name: --output
options.description: The output workspace file path. If not specified, a temporary file will be created

options.name: --no-open
options.description: Skip opening VS Code after creating the workspace [default: False]

options.name: --worktrees-only
options.description: Only include worktrees (exclude main repositories) [default: False]

options.name: --no-worktrees
options.description: Exclude worktrees (only include main repositories) [default: False]
